from manim import *

class Concept02RewardDynamics(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Data and Narration
        voice_text = (
            "In reinforcement learning, the agent learns by flipping pixels and observing the immediate "
            "change in border length. If a flip creates more red-blue boundaries, the agent receives a "
            "positive reward proportional to that increase. If it reduces the boundary, it's penalized."
        )
        
        # 2. Grid Setup (3x3)
        # 0 1 2
        # 3 4 5
        # 6 7 8
        # Center is index 4. Neighbors are 1(up), 3(left), 5(right), 7(down)
        # Setting initial state: neighbor 1 is Blue, others are Red
        colors = [RED, BLUE, RED, RED, RED, RED, RED, RED, RED]
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=1).set_fill(colors[i], opacity=0.8).set_stroke(WHITE, 2)
            for i in range(9)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=3, cols=3, buff=0).scale(1.2).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1.0)

        formula = MathTex(r"R_t = Border_{t} - Border_{t-1}").to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        
        # Manual Bar Chart implementation using Axes and Rectangle (Safer than BarChart class)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 5, 1],
            y_range=[0, 6, 2],
            x_length=4,
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        
        y_label = Text("Reward", font_size=20).next_to(axes.y_axis, UP)
        chart_title = Text("Cumulative Reward", font_size=24).next_to(axes, UP, buff=0.5)

        # 3. Initial Display
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(Create(grid), Create(axes), Write(y_label), Write(chart_title))
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Neighbors and Center Focus
        center_sq = grid[4]
        neighbor_indices = [1, 3, 5, 7]
        highlights = VGroup(*[
            SurroundingRectangle(grid[i], color=YELLOW, buff=0.05) 
            for i in neighbor_indices
        ])
        
        self.play(Create(highlights))
        self.play(Indicate(center_sq, color=YELLOW))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Flip Animation (Red -> Blue)
        # Visual Indicators for Border Changes
        # Red X for border lost at the top (Center was Red, Top is Blue -> Border existed. Now Center becomes Blue -> Lost)
        cross = VGroup(
            Line(UL, DR), Line(UR, DL)
        ).scale(0.2).set_color(RED).move_to(grid[4].get_edge_center(UP))
        
        # Green Lines for borders gained (Left, Right, Bottom neighbors are Red)
        gains = VGroup(
            Line(grid[4].get_corner(UL), grid[4].get_corner(DL), color=GREEN, stroke_width=8),
            Line(grid[4].get_corner(UR), grid[4].get_corner(DR), color=GREEN, stroke_width=8),
            Line(grid[4].get_corner(DL), grid[4].get_corner(DR), color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)
        )
        
        reward_text = Text("+2", color=GREEN).scale(1.2).next_to(grid[4], UR, buff=0.2)

        # Animation: Center flips Red -> Blue
        self.play(
            grid[4].animate.set_fill(BLUE),
            FadeIn(cross),
            Create(gains),
            FadeIn(reward_text, shift=UP),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Update Reward Bar
        # Create a bar manually at the first x-position
        bar1 = Rectangle(
            width=0.5, 
            height=0, 
            fill_opacity=0.8, 
            color=GREEN,
            stroke_width=1
        ).move_to(axes.c2p(1, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)

        self.play(
            bar1.animate.set_height(axes.get_top()[1] - axes.c2p(0, 2)[1], stretch=True).move_to(axes.c2p(1, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN),
            reward_text.animate.scale(0.5).next_to(axes.c2p(1, 2), UP, buff=0.1)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Final Narration Overlay
        narr_box = Rectangle(height=1.5, width=9, fill_color=BLACK, fill_opacity=0.85).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.2)
        narr_text = Text(
            "Spatial arrangement turns into\na sequence of value-driven decisions.",
            font_size=26,
            line_spacing=1.2
        ).move_to(narr_box)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(narr_box), Write(narr_text))
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *

class RewardDynamics(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Header and Formula
        title = Text("Reward Dynamics: Border Length", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        formula = MathTex(r"R_t = \mathrm{Border}_{t} - \mathrm{Border}_{t-1}", font_size=32).next_to(title, DOWN)
        self.add(title, formula)

        # 2. Create a 3x3 grid manually for maximum compatibility
        grid = VGroup()
        for i in range(9):
            sq = Square(side_length=1)
            sq.move_to([i % 3 - 1, -(i // 3) + 1, 0])
            grid.add(sq)
        
        grid.scale(1.2).shift(LEFT * 3)

        # Set initial colors to demonstrate a +2 reward
        # To get +2: 1 boundary initially, 3 boundaries after flip
        # Initial: Center RED, Top RED, Left RED, Right RED, Bottom BLUE
        grid[1].set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)   # Top
        grid[3].set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)   # Left
        grid[5].set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)   # Right
        grid[7].set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.8)  # Bottom
        grid[4].set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)   # Center
        
        # Set stroke and fill for all squares
        for i in range(9):
            if i in [0, 2, 6, 8]:
                grid[i].set_fill(GRAY, opacity=0.2)
                grid[i].set_stroke(GRAY, opacity=0.5)
            else:
                grid[i].set_stroke(WHITE, width=2)
                if i != 4 and i != 7: # If not center or bottom neighbor
                    grid[i].set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)

        self.play(Create(grid))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Focus on center pixel and neighbors
        center = grid[4]
        neighbors = VGroup(grid[1], grid[3], grid[5], grid[7])
        
        focus_rect = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(center, neighbors), color=YELLOW, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(focus_rect))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Highlight the lost border (Initial state: Red-Blue boundary at the bottom)
        lost_border_line = Line(center.get_corner(DL), center.get_corner(DR), color=RED, stroke_width=8)
        self.play(Create(lost_border_line))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Flip center pixel: Red -> Blue
        self.play(
            center.animate.set_fill(BLUE),
            focus_rect.animate.set_color(WHITE),
            run_time=1
        )

        # 6. Create highlights for new borders and a manual cross for the lost one
        nb_top = Line(center.get_corner(UL), center.get_corner(UR), color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)
        nb_left = Line(center.get_corner(UL), center.get_corner(DL), color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)
        nb_right = Line(center.get_corner(UR), center.get_corner(DR), color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)
        
        cp = lost_border_line.get_center()
        l1 = Line(cp + UL*0.2, cp + DR*0.2, color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        l2 = Line(cp + UR*0.2, cp + DL*0.2, color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        manual_cross = VGroup(l1, l2)

        self.play(
            Create(nb_top),
            Create(nb_left),
            Create(nb_right),
            Create(manual_cross),
            FadeOut(lost_border_line, run_time=0.5)
        )

        # 7. Display floating reward text
        reward_text = Text("+2", color=GREEN, font_size=48).next_to(center, RIGHT, buff=0.7)
        self.play(Write(reward_text), reward_text.animate.shift(UP * 0.5))

        # 8. Manual BarChart construction using Axes and Rectangles
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 3, 1], 
            y_range=[0, 4, 1], 
            x_length=3, 
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)
        
        y_axis = axes.get_y_axis()
        unit_height = y_axis.number_to_point(1)[1] - y_axis.number_to_point(0)[1]
        
        # Bar 1: Height 1
        bar1 = Rectangle(
            width=0.6, 
            height=unit_height, 
            fill_opacity=0.8, 
            color=BLUE,
            stroke_width=1
        ).move_to(axes.c2p(1, 0.5))
        
        # Bar 2: Height 3
        bar2 = Rectangle(
            width=0.6, 
            height=unit_height * 3, 
            fill_opacity=0.8, 
            color=GREEN,
            stroke_width=1
        ).move_to(axes.c2p(2, 1.5))
        
        chart_label = Text("Total Borders", font_size=24).next_to(axes, UP)
        label_t_minus_1 = Text("t-1", font_size=18).next_to(bar1, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        label_t = Text("t", font_size=18).next_to(bar2, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        
        self.play(Create(axes), Write(chart_label))
        self.play(FadeIn(bar1), Write(label_t_minus_1))
        self.play(FadeIn(bar2), Write(label_t))
        
        self.wait(2)

        # Cleanup
        self.play(
            FadeOut(grid),
            FadeOut(focus_rect),
            FadeOut(nb_top),
            FadeOut(nb_left),
            FadeOut(nb_right),
            FadeOut(manual_cross),
            FadeOut(reward_text),
            FadeOut(axes),
            FadeOut(bar1),
            FadeOut(bar2),
            FadeOut(chart_label),
            FadeOut(label_t_minus_1),
            FadeOut(label_t),
            FadeOut(title),
            FadeOut(formula)
        )
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *

class Concept02RewardDynamics(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Grid (3x3 pixels)
        # Using Square to represent pixels
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=1).set_stroke(GRAY, 0.5) 
            for _ in range(9)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(3, 3, buff=0.1)
        
        # Initial colors: Center is Red, 3 neighbors Red, 1 neighbor Blue
        # This setup ensures flipping center Red -> Blue creates 2 net new borders
        # (Loses 1 border with the top Blue, gains 3 with the other Red neighbors)
        # Total change: +2
        colors = [
            WHITE, BLUE,  WHITE,
            RED,   RED,   RED,
            WHITE, RED,   WHITE
        ]
        # Only set color for the 3x3 core
        for i, color in enumerate(colors):
            grid[i].set_fill(color, opacity=0.8)

        self.add(grid)

        # 2. Narration Label & Formula
        formula = MathTex("R_t = Border_{t} - Border_{t-1}", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(formula)

        # 3. Focus on Center Pixel and Neighbors
        center_idx = 4
        neighbor_indices = [1, 3, 5, 7] # Up, Left, Right, Down
        
        center_pixel = grid[center_idx]
        neighbors = VGroup(*[grid[i] for i in neighbor_indices])
        
        self.play(Indicate(center_pixel, color=WHITE))
        self.play(neighbors.animate.set_stroke(YELLOW, 4))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Flip Animation and Reward logic
        # Positions for border highlights
        cp = center_pixel.get_center()
        # Top border (Existing boundary between Red and Blue)
        b_top = Line(cp + [-0.5, 0.5, 0], cp + [0.5, 0.5, 0], color=RED, stroke_width=8)
        # Lost border marker
        red_x = Cross(b_top, stroke_color=RED, stroke_width=6).scale(0.2)
        
        # New borders (will appear after flip)
        b_left = Line(cp + [-0.5, -0.5, 0], cp + [-0.5, 0.5, 0], color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)
        b_right = Line(cp + [0.5, -0.5, 0], cp + [0.5, 0.5, 0], color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)
        b_down = Line(cp + [-0.5, -0.5, 0], cp + [0.5, -0.5, 0], color=GREEN, stroke_width=8)

        # Execution
        self.play(center_pixel.animate.set_fill(BLUE))
        
        # Show new borders created and the one lost
        self.play(
            Create(red_x),
            Create(b_left), 
            Create(b_right), 
            Create(b_down),
            run_time=1
        )

        # 5. Floating Reward Text
        reward_text = Text("+2", color=GREEN, font_size=48).next_to(center_pixel, RIGHT, buff=0.6)
        self.play(Write(reward_text), reward_text.animate.shift(UP * 0.5))

        # 6. Manual Bar Chart (Avoiding 'Axes' as it was flagged as disallowed)
        chart_origin = RIGHT * 4.5 + DOWN * 1.5
        x_axis = Line(chart_origin, chart_origin + RIGHT * 2.5)
        y_axis = Line(chart_origin, chart_origin + UP * 3)
        y_label = Text("Reward", font_size=18).next_to(y_axis, UP)
        
        # Bar 1 (Previous step reward)
        bar_t_minus_1 = Rectangle(width=0.4, height=0.8, fill_opacity=0.8, color=BLUE).move_to(chart_origin + RIGHT * 0.6 + UP * 0.4)
        # Bar 2 (Current step reward)
        bar_t = Rectangle(width=0.4, height=2.2, fill_opacity=0.8, color=GREEN).move_to(chart_origin + RIGHT * 1.4 + UP * 1.1)
        
        chart_group = VGroup(x_axis, y_axis, y_label)
        
        self.play(Create(chart_group))
        self.play(FadeIn(bar_t_minus_1))
        self.play(FadeIn(bar_t, shift=UP))

        # Final transition of reward value to chart
        self.play(reward_text.animate.move_to(bar_t.get_top() + UP * 0.3).scale(0.6))

        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class Concept03StateSpaceExplosion(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Define local rate function and color constants to avoid undefined identifier issues
        def ease_in_q(t):
            return t * t

        C_BLUE = "#58C4DD"
        C_RED = "#FC6255"
        C_GRAY = "#888888"

        # 1. Formula Display
        formula = MathTex(r"\text{Total States} = 2^{(N \times N)}").to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.add(formula)

        # 2. Create Coordinate System using NumberPlane (as a fallback for Axes)
        # x-axis: Matrix Size (2 to 6)
        # y-axis: 2^n^2 in log scale (showing the power)
        ax = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[2, 7, 1],
            y_range=[0, 40, 10],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=5,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.2},
            axis_config={"include_numbers": True}
        ).shift(LEFT * 2.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        
        y_label = MathTex(r"\log_2(\text{States})").rotate(90 * DEGREES).next_to(ax, LEFT)
        x_label = Text("Matrix Size (N)", font_size=24).next_to(ax, DOWN)
        
        self.play(Create(ax), FadeIn(y_label), FadeIn(x_label))

        # 3. Initial 2x2 Grid and Q-Table representation
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4).set_fill(C_BLUE, opacity=0.7).set_stroke(WHITE, 1) 
            for _ in range(4)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=2, cols=2, buff=0.1).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.5).shift(UP * 1.5)
        
        grid_title = Text("2x2 Matrix", font_size=24).next_to(grid, UP)

        # Mock Q-Table Frame
        q_table_frame = Rectangle(height=3, width=2, color=WHITE).next_to(grid, DOWN, buff=1)
        q_table_header = Text("Q-Table", font_size=24).next_to(q_table_frame, UP, buff=0.1)
        
        # Initial rows
        rows = VGroup(*[
            Line(LEFT, RIGHT, color=C_GRAY).scale(0.8) 
            for _ in range(12)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).move_to(q_table_frame)

        self.play(
            Create(grid), 
            Write(grid_title), 
            Create(q_table_frame), 
            Create(rows), 
            Write(q_table_header)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. The Growth Animation
        # Define the plotting function explicitly to avoid lambda 'x' issues
        def plot_func(val):
            return val**2

        graph = ax.plot(plot_func, x_range=[2, 5.5], color=C_RED)
        
        # Transition grid to 5x5
        grid_5x5 = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.2).set_fill(C_BLUE if i % 2 == 0 else C_RED, opacity=0.7).set_stroke(WHITE, 0.5) 
            for i in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=5, cols=5, buff=0.05).move_to(grid)
        
        grid_5x5_title = Text("5x5 Matrix", font_size=24).next_to(grid_5x5, UP)

        # Simulate massive row multiplication by scrolling many lines rapidly
        scrolling_rows = VGroup(*[
            Line(LEFT, RIGHT, color=C_GRAY, stroke_width=1).scale(0.8) 
            for _ in range(100)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.04).move_to(q_table_frame.get_bottom(), aligned_edge=DOWN)

        # Animation sequence
        self.play(
            Create(graph),
            Transform(grid, grid_5x5),
            Transform(grid_title, grid_5x5_title),
            scrolling_rows.animate.shift(UP * 10),
            run_time=4,
            rate_func=ease_in_q
        )

        # 5. Representing State Space Explosion
        blur = Rectangle(
            height=3, width=2, 
            fill_opacity=0.9, 
            stroke_width=0
        ).set_fill(color=[C_BLUE, C_RED]).move_to(q_table_frame)
        
        explosion_text = Text("33M+ States", font_size=24, color=WHITE).move_to(blur)
        
        # Curve shoots off the screen
        self.play(
            FadeIn(blur),
            Write(explosion_text),
            graph.animate.shift(UP * 15),
            run_time=1
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class StateSpaceExplosion(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Constructing coordinate system manually to avoid 'Axes'
        horiz_line = Line(LEFT * 3.5 + DOWN * 2, LEFT * 0.5 + DOWN * 2)
        vert_line = Line(LEFT * 3.5 + DOWN * 2, LEFT * 3.5 + UP * 2)
        
        # Adding some ticks manually for visual clarity
        for i in range(5):
            tick_x = -3.5 + i * 0.75
            horiz_line.add(Line(UP*0.1, DOWN*0.1).move_to([tick_x, -2, 0]))
            
        label_n = Text("N (Matrix Size)", font_size=18).next_to(horiz_line, DOWN)
        label_s = Text("Possible States (Log Scale)", font_size=18).rotate(90 * DEGREES).next_to(vert_line, LEFT)
        
        # Reference formula using MathTex
        formula_text = MathTex(r"\text{Total States} = 2^{(N \times N)}", color=YELLOW).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)

        # Q-Table Visualization setup
        table_outline = Rectangle(width=2, height=4, color=WHITE).shift(RIGHT * 3)
        table_label = Text("Q-Table Rows", font_size=22).next_to(table_outline, UP)
        
        # Tracker for the variable N (Size)
        # Using a Dot's y-coordinate as a hidden tracker to avoid 'ValueTracker'
        # We animate N from 2 to 6.
        val_tracker = Dot(point=[0, 2, 0]).hide()
        
        # Graph path using VMobject and updater to avoid 'always_redraw'
        graph_curve = VMobject(color=GREEN).set_stroke(width=4)
        
        def update_my_graph(mob):
            # Extract current value of N from tracker y-coord
            current_n = val_tracker.get_center()[1]
            points_list = []
            # We plot N vs Log2(States) which is N^2
            # Range: N from 2 to current_n
            # x-axis mapping: N=2 -> -3.5, N=6 -> -0.5
            # y-axis mapping: N^2=4 -> -2.0, N^2=36 -> 2.0
            num_steps = 15
            for idx in range(num_steps + 1):
                prog = idx / num_steps
                n_step = 2 + (current_n - 2) * prog
                px = -3.5 + (n_step - 2) * 0.75
                py = -2 + (n_step * n_step - 4) * 0.125
                points_list.append([px, py, 0])
            mob.set_points_as_corners(points_list)
            
        graph_curve.add_updater(update_my_graph)
        
        # Q-Table content logic
        rows_vgroup = VGroup()
        
        def update_table_rows(vg):
            current_n = val_tracker.get_center()[1]
            vg.submobjects = []
            if current_n < 4.8:
                # Discrete line representation
                row_count = int(4 + (current_n - 2) * 12)
                for k in range(row_count):
                    row_line = Line(LEFT, RIGHT, stroke_width=1).set_width(1.8)
                    y_offset = 1.9 - (3.8 * k / (row_count - 1 if row_count > 1 else 1))
                    row_line.move_to(table_outline.get_center() + [0, y_offset, 0])
                    vg.add(row_line)
            else:
                # Explosion representation: a solid blue "blur"
                blur_rect = Rectangle(
                    width=1.9, height=3.9, 
                    fill_color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.8, 
                    stroke_width=0
                ).move_to(table_outline.get_center())
                vg.add(blur_rect)
                # Flickering effect lines
                for m in range(4):
                    noise = Line(LEFT, RIGHT, stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.4)
                    noise.set_width(1.8).move_to(table_outline.get_center() + [0, (m - 1.5) * 0.8, 0])
                    vg.add(noise)

        rows_vgroup.add_updater(update_table_rows)

        # Narrative Caption
        caption = Text("As N grows linearly, states grow exponentially.", font_size=20).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)

        # Initial Display
        self.add(horiz_line, vert_line, label_n, label_s, formula_text, table_outline, table_label)
        self.play(FadeIn(graph_curve), FadeIn(rows_vgroup), Write(caption))
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Main Animation: Explosion of states
        # Defining a simple linear rate function manually to avoid 'linear' identifier
        self.play(
            val_tracker.animate.move_to([0, 6, 0]),
            run_time=8,
            rate_func=lambda alpha: alpha
        )
        
        # Final concluding text
        final_caption = Text("The Q-table becomes too large to compute.", font_size=20, color=RED).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        self.play(Transform(caption, final_caption))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class StateSpaceExplosion(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create Axes (Pattern from Example 3)
        # x-axis: Matrix Size (2 to 6)
        # y-axis: Possible States (log scale)
        # Total States = 2^(N*N) => log2(States) = N^2. We plot n^2 on a linear y-axis.
        ax = Axes(
            x_range=[2, 7, 1],
            y_range=[0, 45, 10],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": True}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)

        xlabel = ax.get_x_axis_label("N (Matrix Size)").scale(0.8)
        ylabel = ax.get_y_axis_label(r"\log_2(\text{States})").scale(0.8)

        # 2. Plotting the function (Pattern from Example 3)
        # Using 'n' instead of 'x' to avoid potential identifier issues
        graph = ax.plot(lambda n: n**2, x_range=[2, 6.2], color=YELLOW)
        
        # 3. Formula reference
        formula = MathTex(r"\text{Total States} = 2^{(N \times N)}").to_edge(UP)

        # 4. Initial 2x2 Grid setup
        # Represents 2^4 = 16 states
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4, fill_opacity=0.5, fill_color=BLUE)
            for _ in range(4)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(2, 2, buff=0.1)
        grid.to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.5).shift(UP * 1.5)
        grid_title = Text("2x2 Grid", font_size=24).next_to(grid, UP)

        # 5. Q-Table Representation
        # A box containing lines representing Q-table rows
        table_bg = Rectangle(width=2.2, height=3.2, color=WHITE).next_to(grid, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        table_rows = VGroup(*[
            Line(LEFT, RIGHT, color=BLUE).set_width(2)
            for _ in range(12)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).move_to(table_bg)
        table_title = Text("Q-Table Rows", font_size=24).next_to(table_bg, UP, buff=0.1)

        # 6. Intro Animations
        self.add(ax, xlabel, ylabel, formula, grid, grid_title, table_bg, table_rows, table_title)
        self.play(Create(graph), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 7. State Space Explosion Animation
        # Target: 5x5 Grid (2^25 states)
        grid_5x5 = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.15, fill_opacity=0.5, fill_color=RED)
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(5, 5, buff=0.05).move_to(grid)
        
        # High-speed scrolling simulation using many dense lines
        fast_rows = VGroup(*[
            Line(LEFT, RIGHT, color=RED, stroke_width=1).set_width(2)
            for _ in range(50)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.03).move_to(table_bg)
        
        # Blur represention of millions of states
        blur_rect = Rectangle(
            width=2.2, height=3.2, 
            fill_opacity=0.9, 
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(table_bg)
        blur_rect.set_fill(color=[BLUE, RED, PURPLE])

        # Execute transformation sequence
        self.play(
            Transform(grid, grid_5x5),
            grid_title.animate.become(Text("5x5 Grid", font_size=24).next_to(grid, UP)),
            table_rows.animate.shift(UP * 4).set_opacity(0),
            FadeIn(fast_rows),
            run_time=3
        )
        
        # Final state: Graph shoots off top, table turns into blur
        self.play(
            FadeIn(blur_rect),
            graph.animate.shift(UP * 10),
            ax.animate.shift(DOWN * 3),
            run_time=2
        )
        
        # Conclusion Narration
        narration = Text(
            "State space grows exponentially with matrix size.",
            font_size=28,
            color=BLUE
        ).to_edge(DOWN)
        
        self.play(Write(narration))
        self.wait(2)

This allows us to handle larger designs, though it can lead to collisions where different patterns share the same table entry, resulting in sub-optimal 'almost-checkered' designs.",

From the box, draw arrows pointing to specific rows in a compact 'Q-Table'. Show two different grids pointing to the same row (a collision) using a flashing red highlight to indicate that the agent might get confused between these two states.",
  "formula": "\\text{Index} = \\text{Hash}(\\text{MatrixState}) \\pmod L",
  "image_ref": "img-6.jpeg"
}

from manim import *

class Concept04HashedQLearning(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula Header
        formula = MathTex(r"\text{Index} = \text{Hash}(\text{MatrixState}) \pmod L")
        formula.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.add(formula)

        # 2. Create three different 4x4 grid patterns using Matrix
        # Pattern 1: Checkerboard
        p1 = [[(i + j) % 2 for i in range(4)] for j in range(4)]
        # Pattern 2: Diagonal blocks
        p2 = [[1 if i < 2 else 0 for i in range(4)] for j in range(4)]
        # Pattern 3: Border pattern
        p3 = [[1 if i == 0 or i == 3 or j == 0 or j == 3 else 0 for i in range(4)] for j in range(4)]

        grid1 = Matrix(p1, h_buff=0.5, v_buff=0.5).scale(0.3)
        grid2 = Matrix(p2, h_buff=0.5, v_buff=0.5).scale(0.3)
        grid3 = Matrix(p3, h_buff=0.5, v_buff=0.5).scale(0.3)

        # Arrange grids on the left
        grid_group = VGroup(grid1, grid2, grid3).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.5).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)

        # 3. Hash Function Box (Central)
        hash_label = Text("Hash Function", font_size=24)
        hash_box_rect = Rectangle(width=3, height=1.5, color=WHITE)
        hash_function = VGroup(hash_box_rect, hash_label).move_to(ORIGIN)

        # 4. Compact Q-Table (Right)
        # Fixed size table with 8 rows
        table_rows = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=2, height=0.4, stroke_width=2) for _ in range(8)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)
        q_table_label = Text("Q-Table", font_size=24).next_to(table_rows, UP)
        q_table = VGroup(table_rows, q_table_label)

        # 5. Define Arrows
        # From Grids to Hash Box
        arrow_g1_to_hash = Arrow(grid1.get_right(), hash_function.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        arrow_g2_to_hash = Arrow(grid2.get_right(), hash_function.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        arrow_g3_to_hash = Arrow(grid3.get_right(), hash_function.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        # From Hash Box to Table Rows
        # Grid 1 and 2 map to the same row (Collision)
        collision_row = table_rows[2]
        normal_row = table_rows[5]

        arrow_hash_to_coll = Arrow(hash_function.get_right(), collision_row.get_left(), buff=0.1, color=RED)
        arrow_hash_to_norm = Arrow(hash_function.get_right(), normal_row.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        # 6. Animation Sequence
        self.play(
            Create(grid_group),
            Create(hash_function),
            Create(q_table),
            rate_func=smooth
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Draw arrows from grids to hash function
        self.play(
            GrowArrow(arrow_g1_to_hash),
            GrowArrow(arrow_g2_to_hash),
            GrowArrow(arrow_g3_to_hash)
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Draw arrows from hash function to table
        # First a normal mapping
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrow_hash_to_norm))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Then the collision mapping
        self.play(
            GrowArrow(arrow_hash_to_coll),
            collision_row.animate.set_fill(RED, opacity=0.3).set_stroke(RED, width=4)
        )

        # Highlight collision using Flash and text
        collision_text = Text("Collision!", color=RED, font_size=20).next_to(collision_row, LEFT, buff=0.2)
        self.play(
            Flash(collision_row, color=RED, flash_radius=0.6),
            Write(collision_text)
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class Concept05DQNAppearance(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Title/Formula
        formula = MathTex(r"Q(s, a; \theta) \approx Q^*(s, a)").to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 1. Create a 5x5 pixel grid
        grid_size = 5
        pixel_grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.5, stroke_width=1, fill_opacity=0.5, fill_color=BLUE)
            for _ in range(grid_size * grid_size)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=grid_size, cols=grid_size, buff=0.1)
        pixel_grid.to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)
        
        grid_label = Text("5x5 State Grid", font_size=24).next_to(pixel_grid, UP)
        
        self.play(Create(pixel_grid), Write(grid_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Flattening into a 1D vector
        flattened_vector = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.2, stroke_width=1, fill_opacity=1, fill_color=BLUE)
            for _ in range(grid_size * grid_size)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05).scale(0.8)
        flattened_vector.move_to(ORIGIN).shift(LEFT * 1.5)

        # Add some 0s and 1s labels to some squares for visualization
        labels = VGroup()
        for i in [0, 5, 12, 18, 24]:
            val = "1" if i % 3 == 0 else "0"
            label = Text(val, font_size=12).move_to(flattened_vector[i].get_center())
            labels.add(label)

        vector_text = Text("Flattened Vector", font_size=20).next_to(flattened_vector, UP)

        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(pixel_grid.copy(), flattened_vector),
            Write(vector_text),
            Write(labels),
            pixel_grid.animate.set_opacity(0.3)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Neural Network Construction
        def create_layer(n, color, position):
            layer = VGroup(*[Circle(radius=0.12, color=color, fill_opacity=0.7) for _ in range(n)])
            layer.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).move_to(position)
            return layer

        input_layer = create_layer(10, WHITE, RIGHT * 0.5) 
        hidden_layer = create_layer(8, WHITE, RIGHT * 2.5)
        output_layer = create_layer(5, GREEN, RIGHT * 4.5)
        
        nn_group = VGroup(input_layer, hidden_layer, output_layer)
        
        # Connections
        connections = VGroup()
        for i in input_layer:
            for j in hidden_layer:
                connections.add(Line(i.get_right(), j.get_left(), stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2))
        for i in hidden_layer:
            for j in output_layer:
                connections.add(Line(i.get_right(), j.get_left(), stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2))

        nn_label = Text("Deep Q-Network", font_size=24).next_to(nn_group, UP)
        
        # Labels for output
        output_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(f"Flip {i+1}", font_size=14).next_to(output_layer[i], RIGHT)
            for i in range(len(output_layer))
        ])

        self.play(
            Create(input_layer),
            Create(hidden_layer),
            Create(output_layer),
            Create(connections),
            Write(nn_label),
            Write(output_labels),
            FadeOut(vector_text),
            FadeOut(labels),
            flattened_vector.animate.next_to(input_layer, LEFT, buff=0.3)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Highlight highest value and flip pixel
        target_index = 2 
        highlight_rect = SurroundingRectangle(output_layer[target_index], color=YELLOW)
        
        # Animate signal flow
        flash_lines = connections.copy().set_color(YELLOW).set_stroke(opacity=1, width=1.5)
        
        # Using a simple successional animation to represent flow since PassingFlash can be version-dependent
        self.play(
            Create(flash_lines, run_time=1.5, rate_func=linear),
        )
        self.play(FadeOut(flash_lines, run_time=0.5))

        self.play(
            Create(highlight_rect),
            output_layer[target_index].animate.set_color(YELLOW).scale(1.3),
            output_labels[target_index].animate.set_color(YELLOW)
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Show the corresponding pixel flipping in original grid
        # Pixel index 12 is roughly the center
        grid_target_index = 12 
        
        self.play(
            pixel_grid[grid_target_index].animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=1).scale(1.2),
            pixel_grid.animate.set_opacity(1)
        )
        self.play(pixel_grid[grid_target_index].animate.scale(1/1.2))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class Concept05DQNApproximation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Pixel Grid Setup
        pixel_grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4, fill_opacity=0.8, fill_color=WHITE, stroke_width=1)
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=5, cols=5, buff=0.1).shift(LEFT * 4)
        
        grid_label = Text("5x5 State Grid", font_size=24).next_to(pixel_grid, UP)
        self.play(Create(pixel_grid), Write(grid_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Flattening the grid into a 1D vector (0s and 1s)
        flattened_vector = VGroup()
        for i in range(25):
            sq = Square(side_length=0.2, fill_opacity=0.8, fill_color=WHITE, stroke_width=0.5)
            # Fill with 0 or 1 strings for visualization
            val = "1" if i % 3 == 0 or i % 7 == 0 else "0"
            txt = Text(val, font_size=12, color=BLACK).move_to(sq.get_center())
            flattened_vector.add(VGroup(sq, txt))
        
        flattened_vector.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05).scale(0.8).shift(LEFT * 1)
        
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(pixel_grid.copy(), flattened_vector),
            grid_label.animate.scale(0.8).next_to(flattened_vector, UP)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Deep Q-Network Visualization (Input -> Hidden -> Output)
        hidden_layer = VGroup(*[Circle(radius=0.15, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.5) for _ in range(8)]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.2).shift(RIGHT * 1.5)
        output_layer = VGroup(*[Circle(radius=0.15, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.5) for _ in range(12)]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).shift(RIGHT * 4)
        
        nn_label = Text("Deep Q-Network", font_size=24).move_to(UP * 3 + RIGHT * 2.5)
        
        # Connections between Flattened Vector (Input) and Hidden Layer
        connections1 = VGroup(*[
            Line(flattened_vector[i].get_right(), hidden_layer[j].get_left(), stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2)
            for i in range(0, 25, 4) for j in range(8)
        ])
        # Connections between Hidden Layer and Output Layer
        connections2 = VGroup(*[
            Line(hidden_layer[i].get_right(), output_layer[j].get_left(), stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2)
            for i in range(8) for j in range(12)
        ])

        self.play(Create(hidden_layer), Create(output_layer), Write(nn_label))
        self.play(Create(connections1), Create(connections2), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Highlight best output and corresponding pixel flip
        target_index = 12 # Middle pixel
        highlight_circle = output_layer[5].copy().set_color(YELLOW).set_stroke(width=4)
        output_text = Text("Max Q-Value: Action 13", font_size=18, color=YELLOW).next_to(output_layer[5], RIGHT)
        
        self.play(
            Create(highlight_circle),
            Write(output_text),
            output_layer[5].animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=1)
        )
        
        # Highlight the pixel in the original 5x5 grid
        pixel_to_flip = pixel_grid[target_index]
        self.play(
            pixel_to_flip.animate.set_fill(RED, opacity=1),
            Flash(pixel_to_flip, color=RED)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Formula and Voiceover text display
        formula = MathTex(r"Q(s, a; \theta) \approx Q^*(s, a)").scale(1.2).to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.2)
        # Replacing constant ITALIC with string "ITALIC" to prevent undefined identifier errors
        voiceover_text = Text(
            "Approximating rewards with a Deep Q-Network.",
            font_size=20,
            slant="ITALIC"
        ).next_to(formula, DOWN, buff=0.3)

        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(FadeIn(voiceover_text))
        self.wait(3)

After the convolutional layers extract features, a Softmax function turns those raw scores into a probability distribution. Essentially, it treats the scores as exponents, then normalizes them so the sum of all categories equals one. This allows the model to pick the most likely material, like distinguishing a pizza box from a newspaper with high confidence.",

Use arrows to show data flow through these layers into a column vector of 20 raw values (MathTex labeled 'x_i'). To the right, animate the Softmax formula appearing. Then, transform the 20 raw values into a horizontal BarChart where the heights represent probabilities. Use 'Indicate' on the highest bar and label it 'Plastic Bottle'.",
  "formula": "softmax(x_{i})=\\frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\\sum_{j=1}^{n}e^{x_{j}}}",
  "image_ref": "img-12.jpeg"
}

from manim import *

class GranularSoftmaxClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Input Image of a plastic bottle
        # Note: If 'img-12.jpeg' is not in the directory, Manim will raise an error.
        # We assume the file exists as per the prompt instructions.
        try:
            bottle = ImageMobject("img-12.jpeg")
        except:
            # Fallback for local testing if image is missing
            bottle = RoundedRectangle(height=3, width=1.5, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.2)
            bottle.add(Text("Plastic Bottle", font_size=15).rotate(90*DEGREES))
            
        bottle.scale(0.7).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        self.add(bottle)

        # 2. CNN Layers
        # Draw a series of vertical rectangles representing CNN layers
        cnn_layers = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=0.25, height=2.0 + (i * 0.2), color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.3)
            for i in range(4)
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.15).next_to(bottle, RIGHT, buff=0.7)
        
        cnn_label = Text("CNN Layers", font_size=20).next_to(cnn_layers, UP)
        
        self.play(
            Create(cnn_layers),
            Write(cnn_label),
            run_time=1.5
        )

        # 3. Column Vector of 20 Raw Values (x_i)
        # Using vector notation to represent 20 values cleanly
        raw_values = MathTex(
            r"x = \begin{bmatrix} x_1 \\ x_2 \\ \vdots \\ x_{20} \end{bmatrix}",
            font_size=36
        ).next_to(cnn_layers, RIGHT, buff=0.8)
        
        arrow_to_x = Arrow(cnn_layers.get_right(), raw_values.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        
        self.play(
            GrowArrow(arrow_to_x),
            Write(raw_values)
        )

        # 4. Softmax Formula
        # softmax(x_{i})=\frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\sum_{j=1}^{n}e^{x_{j}}}
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{softmax}(x_{i}) = \frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\sum_{j=1}^{n} e^{x_{j}}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5).shift(RIGHT * 2)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(formula, shift=DOWN))

        # 5. Horizontal Bar Chart (Probability Distribution)
        # Creating a custom horizontal bar chart using Rectangles
        probabilities = [0.02] * 20
        probabilities[15] = 0.8  # Target index for 'Plastic Bottle'
        probabilities[4] = 0.08
        probabilities[10] = 0.05
        
        bars = VGroup()
        for p in probabilities:
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=p * 4.0 + 0.05, # Map probability to width
                height=0.12,
                fill_opacity=0.8,
                color=BLUE,
                stroke_width=1
            )
            bars.add(bar)
        
        bars.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        bars.next_to(formula, DOWN, buff=0.8).align_to(formula, LEFT)
        
        chart_title = Text("Probabilities", font_size=18).next_to(bars, UP, buff=0.2)

        # Transition: Transform the vector into the bar chart
        self.play(
            Transform(raw_values.copy(), bars),
            FadeIn(bars),
            Write(chart_title)
        )

        # 6. Indicate the highest bar and label it 'Plastic Bottle'
        highest_bar = bars[15]
        result_label = Text("Plastic Bottle", color=YELLOW, font_size=24).next_to(highest_bar, RIGHT, buff=0.3)
        
        self.play(
            Indicate(highest_bar, color=YELLOW, scale_factor=1.2),
            Create(result_label)
        )

        self.wait(3)

They manually segmented single objects\u2014like a can or a piece of cardboard\u2014and then used a script to randomly sample, scale, and rotate them onto a blank canvas. By overlapping these images at different angles, they forced the model to learn how to identify objects even when they are partially hidden or tilted, mimicking the messy reality of a recycling bin.",
  "goal": "Visualize the process of creating a synthetic multi-object dataset using rotation, scaling, and layering.",
  "remarks": "Start with a blank white Rectangle representing the canvas. Display five segmented trash icons (e.g., a bottle, a can, a paper scrap) floating around it. Use a ValueTracker to show random numbers for 'scale' and 'rotation'. One by one, animate each icon moving onto the canvas, applying 'Rotate' and 'Scale' transforms as they land. Ensure some icons 'overlap' by placing them on different Z-indices, creating a crowded scene of 9 objects.",
  "formula": null,
  "image_ref": "img-0.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.

from manim import *

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Blank canvas represented by a white rectangle
        canvas = Rectangle(width=8, height=6, color=WHITE)
        canvas.set_fill(WHITE, opacity=0.1)
        self.add(canvas)

        # 2. UI Labels for Scale and Rotation (Avoid ValueTracker due to restrictions)
        scale_label = Text("Scale: 1.00", font_size=24).to_corner(UL, buff=0.5)
        rot_label = Text("Rotation: 0°", font_size=24).next_to(scale_label, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        self.add(scale_label, rot_label)

        # 3. Define trash icon shapes and standard colors
        colors = [YELLOW, RED, GREEN, BLUE, ORANGE, PURPLE, GOLD, WHITE, GRAY]
        
        icons = []
        for i in range(9):
            c = colors[i % len(colors)]
            shape_type = i % 3
            if shape_type == 0:
                icon = Circle(radius=0.4, color=c, fill_opacity=0.8)
            elif shape_type == 1:
                icon = Square(side_length=0.7, color=c, fill_opacity=0.8)
            else:
                icon = Triangle(color=c, fill_opacity=0.8).scale(0.6)
            
            # Position first 5 floating around the canvas
            if i < 5:
                # Deterministic pseudo-random positions to avoid external dependencies
                x_pos = -5 if i % 2 == 0 else 5
                y_pos = (i - 2) * 1.5
                icon.move_to([x_pos, y_pos, 0])
                self.add(icon)
            icons.append(icon)

        # 4. Animate each icon moving onto the canvas with rotation and scaling
        for i, item in enumerate(icons):
            # If not already on screen, start from the bottom
            if i >= 5:
                item.move_to([0, -4.5, 0])
                self.add(item)
            
            # Deterministic variation for "random" scale, rotation, and position
            s_val = 0.6 + (i * 0.13) % 1.0
            r_val = i * 40
            target_x = ((i * 1.7) % 6) - 3
            target_y = ((i * 1.1) % 4) - 2
            
            # Create updated labels for this step
            new_scale_text = Text(f"Scale: {s_val:.2f}", font_size=24).to_corner(UL, buff=0.5)
            new_rot_text = Text(f"Rotation: {int(r_val)}°", font_size=24).next_to(new_scale_text, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
            
            # Animate movement, scaling, and rotation simultaneously
            # Objects added later in the loop naturally have higher Z-indices (overlap)
            self.play(
                item.animate.scale(s_val).rotate(r_val * DEGREES).move_to([target_x, target_y, 0]),
                Transform(scale_label, new_scale_text),
                Transform(rot_label, new_rot_text),
                run_time=0.6
            )

        # 5. Hold final scene showing the synthetic pile
        self.wait(2)

  "voice": "To find these objects in a pile, YOLOv10 divides the image into a grid. If the center of a piece of trash falls into a cell, that cell is responsible for predicting a bounding box. The model's loss function specifically penalizes errors in the box coordinates and the square roots of the width and height. By using square roots, the model ensures that a small error in a tiny object is penalized more heavily than the same error in a large object, which is crucial for finding small scraps of paper amidst larger debris.",

Draw a 'SurroundingRectangle' (the predicted box) and a slightly offset dashed rectangle (the ground truth). Animate arrows showing the distance between their centers (x, y) and the difference in their dimensions (w, h). Show the LaTeX loss formula and highlight the coordinate terms as the boxes align during a mock 'training' animation.",
  "formula": "\\lambda_{coord}\\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}\\left((x_{i}-\\hat{x}_{i})^{2}+(y_{i}-\\hat{y}_{i})^{2}\\right) + \\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}\\left((\\sqrt{w_{i}}-\\sqrt{\\hat{w}_{i}})^{2}+(\\sqrt{h_{i}}-\\sqrt{\\hat{h}_{i}})^{2}\\right)",

from manim import *

class YOLOv10LocalizationLoss(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Background image placeholder or actual image
        try:
            image = ImageMobject("img-11.jpeg").scale_to_fit_height(6)
            self.add(image)
        except:
            bg_rect = Rectangle(width=10, height=6, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=0.1)
            self.add(bg_rect)

        # 2. 7x7 Grid (Manually constructed as NumberPlane is disallowed)
        grid = VGroup()
        for i in range(8):
            # Vertical lines
            v_line = Line(start=[i-3.5, -3.5, 0], end=[i-3.5, 3.5, 0], stroke_width=1, stroke_opacity=0.4, color=WHITE)
            grid.add(v_line)
            # Horizontal lines
            h_line = Line(start=[-3.5, i-3.5, 0], end=[3.5, i-3.5, 0], stroke_width=1, stroke_opacity=0.4, color=WHITE)
            grid.add(h_line)
        self.play(Create(grid))

        # 3. Highlight specific cell (approximate center for a soda can)
        # Cell centered at (-1, 1)
        target_cell = Rectangle(width=1, height=1, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=5)
        target_cell.move_to([-1, 1, 0])
        self.play(Create(target_cell))

        # 4. Predicted Box (Green) and Ground Truth Box (Red)
        # We use standard Rectangle as DashedVMobject is disallowed
        gt_box = Rectangle(width=1.4, height=2.0, color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        gt_box.move_to([-1, 1, 0])
        gt_box.set_stroke(opacity=0.6)
        
        pred_box = Rectangle(width=1.9, height=2.6, color=GREEN, stroke_width=4)
        pred_box.move_to([-1.3, 1.3, 0])
        
        self.play(Create(gt_box), Create(pred_box))

        # 5. Delta arrows for center (x, y)
        p_center = pred_box.get_center()
        g_center = gt_box.get_center()
        
        # x-offset arrow
        arrow_x = Line(start=p_center, end=[g_center[0], p_center[1], 0], color=BLUE)
        arrow_x.add_tip(tip_length=0.15)
        # y-offset arrow
        arrow_y = Line(start=[g_center[0], p_center[1], 0], end=g_center, color=BLUE)
        arrow_y.add_tip(tip_length=0.15)
        
        # Dimension difference visualizer (w, h)
        w_diff_line = Line(
            start=pred_box.get_corner(DR) + 0.1 * DOWN,
            end=pred_box.get_corner(DR) + 0.1 * DOWN + (gt_box.get_width() - pred_box.get_width()) * RIGHT,
            color=ORANGE
        )
        w_diff_line.add_tip(tip_length=0.1)

        self.play(Create(arrow_x), Create(arrow_y))
        self.play(Create(w_diff_line))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 6. LaTeX Loss Formula (Self-contained and robust)
        # Using the formula provided in the reference
        formula_str = r"\lambda_{coord}\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}\left((x_{i}-\hat{x}_{i})^{2}+(y_{i}-\hat{y}_{i})^{2}\right) + \sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}\left((\sqrt{w_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{w}_{i}})^{2}+(\sqrt{h_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{h}_{i}})^{2}\right)"
        loss_formula = MathTex(formula_str).scale(0.5).to_edge(UP, buff=0.3)
        
        # Manual background for the formula text
        formula_bg = Rectangle(
            width=loss_formula.get_width() + 0.4,
            height=loss_formula.get_height() + 0.4,
            fill_color=BLACK,
            fill_opacity=0.8,
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(loss_formula)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(formula_bg), Write(loss_formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Mock Training Animation
        # Boxes align and formula highlights
        self.play(
            pred_box.animate.move_to(g_center).set_width(1.4).set_height(2.0),
            FadeOut(arrow_x),
            FadeOut(arrow_y),
            FadeOut(w_diff_line),
            loss_formula.animate.set_color(YELLOW),
            run_time=2.5
        )
        self.wait(2)

{
  "id": "concept-04-selective-layer-activation",
  "voice": "The breakthrough in efficiency came from 'targeted' fine-tuning. Instead of retraining the whole YOLO model, the team found that the early layers\u2014the backbone\u2014already knew how to see basic shapes. By freezing most of the model and only 'activating' the high-level semantic layers, specifically layers 7 through 10 and the final classification head at layer 23, they achieved 86 percent accuracy. It\u2019s like keeping a person\u2019s general vision intact but specifically training their brain to recognize the nuances of 20 types of garbage.",
  "goal": "Visualize the strategy of freezing specific model layers to optimize training performance.",
  "remarks": "Create a horizontal chain of 24 'Square' objects representing layers 0 to 23. Color layers 0-6 gray (labeled 'Frozen Backbone'). Color layers 7-10 bright blue with a 'Glow' effect (labeled 'Active Semantics'). Color layers 11-22 gray. Color layer 23 bright orange (labeled 'Active Head'). Animate a 'Succession' of pulses moving through the blue and orange squares while the gray ones remain static, representing gradients updating only in selected regions.",
  "formula": null,
  "image_ref": "img-10.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.
generated_code:
from manim import *

class Concept04SelectiveLayerActivation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create a horizontal chain of 24 'Square' objects representing layers 0 to 23
        layers = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.35, fill_opacity=0.6) 
            for _ in range(24)
        ])
        layers.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.15)
        layers.move_to(UP * 1.5)

        # Color layers 0-6 gray (labeled 'Frozen Backbone')
        for i in range(0, 7):
            layers[i].set_color(GRAY)
        
        # Color layers 7-10 bright blue with a 'Glow' effect (labeled 'Active Semantics')
        for i in range(7, 11):
            layers[i].set_color(BLUE)
            layers[i].set_stroke(BLUE, width=6)
        
        # Color layers 11-22 gray
        for i in range(11, 23):
            layers[i].set_color(GRAY)
        
        # Color layer 23 bright orange (labeled 'Active Head')
        layers[23].set_color(ORANGE)
        layers[23].set_stroke(ORANGE, width=6)

        # Labels
        frozen_label = Text("Frozen Backbone", font_size=20, color=GRAY).next_to(layers[0:7], DOWN, buff=0.4)
        active_sem_label = Text("Active Semantics", font_size=20, color=BLUE).next_to(layers[7:11], UP, buff=0.4)
        active_head_label = Text("Active Head", font_size=20, color=ORANGE).next_to(layers[23], UP, buff=0.4)

        # Narration text (Replaced Paragraph with Text and line breaks for compatibility)
        narration_str = (
            "The breakthrough in efficiency came from 'targeted' fine-tuning.\n"
            "Instead of retraining the whole YOLO model, the team froze\n"
            "the backbone and only activated high-level semantic layers (7-10)\n"
            "and the classification head (23), achieving 86% accuracy."
        )
        narration = Text(narration_str, font_size=24, line_spacing=0.8).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)

        # Try to load image as mentioned in prompt
        try:
            image = ImageMobject("img-10.jpeg")
            image.scale(1.2).move_to(ORIGIN)
            self.add(image)
        except:
            # If image missing, continue without it
            pass

        # Add components to scene
        self.add(layers, frozen_label, active_sem_label, active_head_label, narration)

        # Define pulses for layers 7, 8, 9, 10, and 23
        active_indices = [7, 8, 9, 10, 23]
        pulses = [
            Flash(
                layers[i], 
                color=layers[i].get_color(), 
                flash_radius=0.4, 
                line_length=0.2,
                run_time=0.5
            ) 
            for i in active_indices
        ]

        # Animate a 'Succession' of pulses moving through the selected squares
        self.play(Succession(*pulses))
        
        # Continuous animation of pulses to represent "gradients updating"
        self.play(
            AnimationGroup(
                *[Indicate(layers[i], scale_factor=1.2, color=layers[i].get_color()) for i in active_indices],
                run_time=2,
                lag_ratio=0.2
            )
        )

        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class Concept01DominantZoneMechanism(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Colors (using hex to avoid undefined identifier errors)
        C_LIGHT_BLUE = "#ADD8E6"
        C_DEEP_BLUE = "#00008B"
        C_GREEN = "#008000"
        C_ORANGE = "#FFA500"
        C_RED = "#FF0000"
        C_CHILLED = "#E0FFFF"
        C_SUPPLY_BLUE = "#0000FF"

        # 1. Initialize the AHU (Central Air Handler) at the bottom
        ahu = Rectangle(width=3.0, height=1.5, color=C_LIGHT_BLUE, fill_opacity=0.5)
        ahu.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.0)
        ahu_label = Text("AHU", font_size=24).move_to(ahu.get_center())
        ahu_group = VGroup(ahu, ahu_label)

        # 2. Initialize five zones at the top
        zones = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=1.5, height=1.2, color=C_GREEN, fill_opacity=0.8) 
            for _ in range(5)
        ])
        zones.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.4)
        zones.to_edge(UP, buff=1.0)
        
        zone_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(f"Zone {i+1}", font_size=20).move_to(zones[i].get_center())
            for i in range(5)
        ])

        self.add(ahu_group, zones, zone_labels)
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Animate heat gradient growing in two "dominant" zones (Zone 2 and Zone 4)
        dominant_indices = [1, 3]
        self.play(
            *[zones[i].animate.set_fill(C_ORANGE) for i in dominant_indices],
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Cooling requests (dashed lines with tips) from orange zones to AHU
        requests = VGroup()
        for i in dominant_indices:
            req = DashedLine(zones[i].get_bottom(), ahu.get_top(), color=C_RED, dash_length=0.1)
            req.add_tip(tip_length=0.2)
            requests.add(req)

        request_label = Text("Cooling Request", color=C_RED, font_size=20).next_to(requests, RIGHT)

        self.play(Create(requests), Write(request_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. AHU changes to deep blue as it responds
        self.play(
            ahu.animate.set_fill(C_DEEP_BLUE, opacity=0.9).set_stroke(C_DEEP_BLUE),
            FadeOut(requests),
            FadeOut(request_label),
            run_time=1
        )

        # 6. Supply Air arrows flowing back to ALL zones
        supply_arrows = VGroup()
        for i in range(5):
            arr = Arrow(ahu.get_top(), zones[i].get_bottom(), color=C_SUPPLY_BLUE, buff=0.1)
            supply_arrows.add(arr)
            
        supply_label = Text("Supply Air (Colder)", color=C_SUPPLY_BLUE, font_size=20).next_to(supply_arrows, DOWN, buff=0.5).shift(UP * 2)

        self.play(Create(supply_arrows), Write(supply_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Green zones turn "chilled" white-blue (overcooling)
        overcooled_indices = [0, 2, 4]
        
        self.play(
            *[zones[i].animate.set_fill(C_CHILLED) for i in overcooled_indices],
            *[zones[i].animate.set_fill(C_GREEN) for i in dominant_indices], # Dominant zones satisfied
            run_time=2
        )
        
        overcool_text = Text("Overcooled", color=C_LIGHT_BLUE, font_size=24).move_to(zones[0].get_top() + UP*0.4)
        self.play(Write(overcool_text))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class Concept01DominantZoneMechanism(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Colors and Constants
        COLOR_AHU_START = BLUE
        COLOR_AHU_END = DARK_BLUE
        COLOR_ZONE_NORMAL = GREEN
        COLOR_ZONE_HOT = ORANGE
        COLOR_ZONE_OVERCOOLED = "#E0FFFF" # Chilled white-blue
        
        # 2. Create the central AHU rectangle
        ahu = Rectangle(width=3, height=2, color=COLOR_AHU_START, fill_opacity=1)
        ahu_label = Text("AHU", color=WHITE, font_size=32).move_to(ahu.get_center())
        ahu_group = VGroup(ahu, ahu_label)
        
        # 3. Create five smaller rectangles representing 'Zones'
        # Position them around the central AHU
        zones = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=1.8, height=1.2, color=COLOR_ZONE_NORMAL, fill_opacity=1)
            for _ in range(5)
        ])
        
        # Layout: 2 on the right (will be dominant), 3 on the left (will be overcooled)
        zones[0].move_to(4.5 * RIGHT + 1.5 * UP)    # Dominant Zone 1
        zones[1].move_to(4.5 * RIGHT + 1.5 * DOWN)  # Dominant Zone 2
        zones[2].move_to(4.5 * LEFT + 2.2 * UP)    # Normal Zone 3
        zones[3].move_to(4.5 * LEFT + 0 * UP)       # Normal Zone 4
        zones[4].move_to(4.5 * LEFT + 2.2 * DOWN)  # Normal Zone 5
        
        zone_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(f"Zone {i+1}", font_size=24).next_to(zones[i], UP, buff=0.1)
            for i in range(5)
        ])

        self.add(ahu_group, zones, zone_labels)
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Animate a heat gradient (orange) growing in two zones
        self.play(
            zones[0].animate.set_fill(COLOR_ZONE_HOT),
            zones[1].animate.set_fill(COLOR_ZONE_HOT),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Show dashed arrows from orange zones to the AHU
        # Using DashedLine with a tip added because DashedArrow is not a standard class
        req_arrow1 = DashedLine(zones[0].get_left(), ahu.get_right(), color=RED).add_tip()
        req_arrow2 = DashedLine(zones[1].get_left(), ahu.get_right(), color=RED).add_tip()
        req_text = Text("Cooling Request", color=RED, font_size=28).next_to(ahu, UP, buff=0.5)
        
        self.play(
            Create(req_arrow1),
            Create(req_arrow2),
            Write(req_text),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 6. Change AHU color to indicate response
        self.play(
            ahu.animate.set_fill(COLOR_AHU_END),
            FadeOut(req_text),
            run_time=1
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 7. Show 'Supply Air' arrows flowing back to ALL zones
        supply_arrows = VGroup(*[
            DashedLine(ahu.get_center(), zones[i].get_center(), color=COLOR_AHU_END).add_tip()
            for i in range(5)
        ])
        supply_text = Text("Supply Air", color=COLOR_AHU_END, font_size=28).next_to(ahu, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        self.play(
            FadeOut(req_arrow1),
            FadeOut(req_arrow2),
            Create(supply_arrows),
            Write(supply_text),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 8. The green zones turn a 'chilled' white-blue color to signify overcooling
        self.play(
            zones[2].animate.set_fill(COLOR_ZONE_OVERCOOLED),
            zones[3].animate.set_fill(COLOR_ZONE_OVERCOOLED),
            zones[4].animate.set_fill(COLOR_ZONE_OVERCOOLED),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class DominantZoneMechanism(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Define specific hex colors to avoid undefined identifier errors
        color_ahu_light = "#87CEEB" # Light Blue
        color_ahu_deep = "#00008B"  # Deep Blue
        color_zone_green = "#228B22" # Forest Green
        color_zone_heat = "#FF8C00"  # Dark Orange
        color_zone_chilled = "#E0FFFF" # Light Cyan / White-Blue
        color_white = "#FFFFFF"

        # 1. Setup AHU and Zones
        # AHU at the top
        ahu_rect = Rectangle(width=3, height=1.5, color=color_ahu_light, fill_opacity=0.8)
        ahu_label = Text("AHU", font_size=24, color=color_white).move_to(ahu_rect.get_center())
        ahu = VGroup(ahu_rect, ahu_label).to_edge(UP, buff=0.8)

        # 5 Zones at the bottom
        zones = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Rectangle(width=2.0, height=1.2, color=color_zone_green, fill_opacity=0.6),
                Text(f"Zone {i+1}", font_size=18, color=color_white)
            ) for i in range(5)
        ])
        zones.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.4).to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.2)

        self.add(ahu, zones)
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Heat gradient growing in two dominant zones (Zone 1 and Zone 2)
        dominant_indices = [0, 1]
        self.play(
            *[zones[i][0].animate.set_fill(color_zone_heat, opacity=0.9).set_stroke(color_zone_heat) 
              for i in dominant_indices],
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Cooling request arrows (Dashed) moving from orange zones to AHU
        req_arrows = VGroup()
        for i in dominant_indices:
            # Create a dashed line and add a tip to make it an arrow
            line = DashedLine(
                start=zones[i].get_top(), 
                end=ahu_rect.get_bottom(), 
                color=color_zone_heat,
                dash_length=0.15
            )
            line.add_tip(tip_length=0.2)
            req_arrows.add(line)
        
        req_label = Text("Cooling Request", color=color_zone_heat, font_size=20).next_to(req_arrows, RIGHT, buff=0.5)

        self.play(Create(req_arrows))
        self.play(Write(req_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. AHU changes color to deep blue
        self.play(
            ahu_rect.animate.set_color(color_ahu_deep).set_fill(color_ahu_deep),
            FadeOut(req_arrows),
            FadeOut(req_label),
            run_time=1
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 5. Deep blue 'Supply Air' arrows flowing back to ALL zones
        supply_arrows = VGroup()
        for i in range(5):
            arr = Arrow(
                start=ahu_rect.get_bottom(), 
                end=zones[i].get_top(), 
                color=color_ahu_deep, 
                buff=0.1,
                stroke_width=5
            )
            supply_arrows.add(arr)
        
        supply_label = Text("Supply Air (Chilled)", color=color_ahu_deep, font_size=20).next_to(supply_arrows, RIGHT, buff=0.1)

        self.play(Create(supply_arrows))
        self.play(Write(supply_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Green zones (non-dominant) turn white-blue to signify overcooling
        other_indices = [2, 3, 4]
        self.play(
            *[zones[i][0].animate.set_fill(color_zone_chilled, opacity=0.8).set_color(color_zone_chilled) 
              for i in other_indices],
            FadeOut(supply_arrows),
            FadeOut(supply_label),
            run_time=2
        )

        # Final explanatory text
        explanation = Text(
            "Majority of building overcooled to satisfy dominant zones.",
            font_size=24,
            color=color_white
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.3)
        
        self.play(Write(explanation))
        self.wait(3)

  "id": "concept-02-logistic-regression-features",
  "voice": "To predict which zones will be dominant tomorrow, we use a logistic regression model for every single zone. Instead of looking at raw spikes, we look at the 'memory' of the zone. We calculate two main features: the average temperature gap from the setpoint over the last 24 hours, and the average over the last 30 days. This allows the model to distinguish between a temporary door being left open and a zone that is fundamentally struggling with its cooling load.",
  "goal": "Visualize the mathematical inputs and the logistic mapping used for prediction.",
  "remarks": "Display a NumberPlane. On the right, show a sigmoid curve. Create two sliding bars on the left labeled '1-Day T-CSP' and '30-Day T-CSP'. As the bars slide up and down, animate a dot moving along the sigmoid curve. Use TransformMatchingTex to show how the feature vector X scales the weights in the logistic formula to move the probability P(y=1) between 0 and 1.",
  "formula": "P(y=1) = \\frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\\beta_0 + \\beta_1 X_{1d} + \\beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
  "image_ref": "img-10.jpeg"
}

Also, use this formula as a reference:
P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d})}}

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Sigmoid Math Function
        def sigmoid_calc(val_z):
            return 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-val_z))

        # 2. Coordinate System
        # Using NumberPlane as requested in remarks
        plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 2],
            y_range=[0, 1.2, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        
        # Sigmoid Curve
        sigmoid_curve = plane.plot(sigmoid_calc, color=BLUE)
        
        # 3. Trackers for Features
        # ValueTracker is used in Reference Example 2
        tracker_1d = ValueTracker(0.0)
        tracker_30d = ValueTracker(0.0)
        
        # 4. Sliders (Left Side)
        line_1d = Line(DOWN, UP).scale(1.5).shift(LEFT * 4.5 + UP * 1.2)
        line_30d = Line(DOWN, UP).scale(1.5).shift(LEFT * 4.5 + DOWN * 1.2)
        
        dot_1d = Dot(color=YELLOW)
        dot_30d = Dot(color=YELLOW)
        
        # Using named functions for updaters to avoid potential lambda issues
        def update_slider_1d(mob):
            val = tracker_1d.get_value()
            # Map range [-3, 3] to [0, 1] proportion
            prop = (val + 3.0) / 6.0
            if prop < 0: prop = 0
            if prop > 1: prop = 1
            mob.move_to(line_1d.point_from_proportion(prop))
            
        def update_slider_30d(mob):
            val = tracker_30d.get_value()
            prop = (val + 3.0) / 6.0
            if prop < 0: prop = 0
            if prop > 1: prop = 1
            mob.move_to(line_30d.point_from_proportion(prop))
            
        dot_1d.add_updater(update_slider_1d)
        dot_30d.add_updater(update_slider_30d)
        
        label_1d = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=20).next_to(line_1d, UP)
        label_30d = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=20).next_to(line_30d, UP)
        
        # 5. Prediction Dot on Curve
        prediction_dot = Dot(color=RED)
        
        def update_prediction_dot(mob):
            z_score = tracker_1d.get_value() + tracker_30d.get_value()
            # Clamp z_score to plane range
            if z_score < -6: z_score = -6
            if z_score > 6: z_score = 6
            y_prob = sigmoid_calc(z_score)
            mob.move_to(plane.c2p(z_score, y_prob))
        
        prediction_dot.add_updater(update_prediction_dot)
        
        # 6. Formulas
        formula_initial = MathTex(
            r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.6)
        
        # 7. Rendering and Animation
        self.add(plane, sigmoid_curve, line_1d, line_30d, dot_1d, dot_30d, label_1d, label_30d, prediction_dot, formula_initial)
        
        # Move sliders and animate dot
        self.play(tracker_1d.animate.set_value(2.5))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(tracker_30d.animate.set_value(-1.5))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show final scaling
        formula_scaled = MathTex(
            r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(0 + 1(2.0) + 1(1.0))}}",
            font_size=32
        ).move_to(formula_initial)
        
        self.play(
            tracker_1d.animate.set_value(2.0),
            tracker_30d.animate.set_value(1.0)
        )
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(formula_initial, formula_scaled))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Plane and Sigmoid Curve (Avoiding 'Axes' as requested)
        plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 2],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        
        # Sigmoid function: 1 / (1 + exp(-z))
        sigmoid_curve = plane.plot(
            lambda x: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x)),
            color=YELLOW
        )
        
        curve_label = MathTex("P(y=1) = \\sigma(z)").next_to(plane, UP, buff=0.2)

        # 2. Mathematical Formulas (Avoiding 'TransformMatchingTex')
        formula_initial = MathTex(
            "P(y=1) = \\frac{1}{1 + e^{-z}}"
        ).scale(0.8).to_edge(UP).shift(LEFT * 2.5)
        
        formula_expanded = MathTex(
            "P(y=1) = \\frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\\beta_0 + \\beta_1 X_{1d} + \\beta_{30} X_{30d})}}"
        ).scale(0.8).to_edge(UP).shift(LEFT * 2.5)

        # 3. Vertical Sliders (Mimicking input features X_1d and X_30d)
        # Using Dot positions to track values instead of 'ValueTracker'
        slider1_line = Line(DOWN, UP).scale(1.5).shift(LEFT * 5.5)
        slider1_dot = Dot(color=BLUE).move_to(slider1_line.get_center())
        slider1_label = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=18).next_to(slider1_line, DOWN)

        slider30_line = Line(DOWN, UP).scale(1.5).shift(LEFT * 4.0)
        slider30_dot = Dot(color=BLUE).move_to(slider30_line.get_center())
        slider30_label = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=18).next_to(slider30_line, DOWN)

        # 4. Prediction Dot on the Sigmoid Curve
        # Logistic weights constants
        B0, B1, B30 = -0.5, 1.2, 0.8
        
        prediction_dot = Dot(color=RED, radius=0.12)
        
        def update_prediction(m):
            # Calculate feature values from slider positions (range approx -2 to 2)
            x1 = (slider1_dot.get_center()[1] - slider1_line.get_center()[1]) / 1.5 * 3
            x30 = (slider30_dot.get_center()[1] - slider30_line.get_center()[1]) / 1.5 * 3
            
            # Calculate logit z
            z = B0 + B1 * x1 + B30 * x30
            # Map z to the plane and sigmoid value
            y = 1 / (1 + np.exp(-z))
            m.move_to(plane.c2p(z, y))

        prediction_dot.add_updater(update_prediction)

        # 5. Animation Sequence
        self.play(Create(plane), Create(sigmoid_curve), Write(curve_label))
        self.play(Write(formula_initial))
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Transform formula to show parameters
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(formula_initial, formula_expanded))
        self.wait(1)

        # Show Sliders
        self.play(
            Create(slider1_line), Create(slider1_dot), Write(slider1_label),
            Create(slider30_line), Create(slider30_dot), Write(slider30_label)
        )
        
        # Add the interactive dot
        self.add(prediction_dot)
        self.wait(1)

        # Animate Sliders to show how features affect probability
        # Moving 1-Day gap up (Higher cooling load memory)
        self.play(
            slider1_dot.animate.move_to(slider1_line.get_top()),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Moving 30-Day gap up
        self.play(
            slider30_dot.animate.move_to(slider30_line.get_top()),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Moving both down (Low cooling load memory)
        self.play(
            slider1_dot.animate.move_to(slider1_line.get_bottom()),
            slider30_dot.animate.move_to(slider30_line.get_bottom()),
            run_time=3
        )
        self.wait(2)

        # Final return to center
        self.play(
            slider1_dot.animate.move_to(slider1_line.get_center()),
            slider30_dot.animate.move_to(slider30_line.get_center()),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Weights for the logistic model
        weight_0 = -2.0
        weight_1 = 0.5
        weight_30 = 0.5

        # Manual Coordinate System setup (to avoid restricted Axes/NumberPlane)
        origin_ref = RIGHT * 3 + DOWN * 1.5
        horiz_line = Line(origin_ref + LEFT * 3, origin_ref + RIGHT * 3, color=WHITE)
        vert_line = Line(origin_ref, origin_ref + UP * 3.5, color=WHITE)
        horiz_label = Text("Logit Value (z)", font_size=16).next_to(horiz_line, DOWN)
        vert_label = Text("Probability P(y=1)", font_size=16).next_to(vert_line, LEFT).rotate(90 * DEGREES)
        self.add(horiz_line, vert_line, horiz_label, vert_label)

        # Helper function to map math values to scene positions
        def get_sigmoid_pos(z_in):
            # Standard sigmoid formula
            p_out = 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-z_in))
            # x-position: maps z from [-4, 4] to 6 units of screen width
            # y-position: maps prob from [0, 1] to 3 units of screen height
            return origin_ref + RIGHT * (z_in * 0.75) + UP * (p_out * 3)

        # Draw the curve manually using a series of points
        curve_steps = np.linspace(-4, 4, 50)
        curve_points = [get_sigmoid_pos(val) for val in curve_steps]
        logistic_curve = VMobject(color=RED)
        logistic_curve.set_points_as_corners(curve_points)
        self.add(logistic_curve)

        # Manual Sliders on the left side
        # Slider for 1-Day T-CSP
        path_1d = Line(LEFT * 6 + UP * 1, LEFT * 3 + UP * 1)
        knob_1d = Dot(path_1d.get_start(), color=GREEN)
        label_1d = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=20).next_to(path_1d, UP)
        
        # Slider for 30-Day T-CSP
        path_30d = Line(LEFT * 6 + DOWN * 1, LEFT * 3 + DOWN * 1)
        knob_30d = Dot(path_30d.get_start(), color=BLUE)
        label_30d = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=20).next_to(path_30d, UP)
        
        self.add(path_1d, knob_1d, label_1d, path_30d, knob_30d, label_30d)

        # Prediction Dot that follows the sigmoid curve
        res_dot = Dot(color=YELLOW)
        
        def refresh_res_dot(mob):
            # Convert knob horizontal position to a feature value (0 to 10 scale)
            # Path width is 3.0 units
            feat_1 = (knob_1d.get_center()[0] - path_1d.get_start()[0]) / 3.0 * 10
            feat_30 = (knob_30d.get_center()[0] - path_30d.get_start()[0]) / 3.0 * 10
            
            # Weighted sum for the exponent
            logit_z = weight_0 + weight_1 * feat_1 + weight_30 * feat_30
            
            # Map the calculated logit to a point on our visual curve
            # Clamping prevents the dot from flying off the drawn segment
            clamped_z = max(min(logit_z, 4), -4)
            mob.move_to(get_sigmoid_pos(clamped_z))

        res_dot.add_updater(refresh_res_dot)
        self.add(res_dot)

        # Logic Formula at the top
        formula_tex = MathTex(
            r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.add(formula_tex)

        # Animation Sequence
        # 1. Slide the 1-Day feature up
        self.play(knob_1d.animate.move_to(path_1d.point_from_proportion(0.85)), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 2. Slide the 30-Day feature up
        self.play(knob_30d.animate.move_to(path_30d.point_from_proportion(0.75)), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Highlight the formula to show scaling
        glow_box = SurroundingRectangle(formula_tex, color=YELLOW, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(glow_box))
        self.play(FadeOut(glow_box))
        
        # 4. Slide back to show the range of probabilities
        self.play(
            knob_1d.animate.move_to(path_1d.point_from_proportion(0.1)),
            knob_30d.animate.move_to(path_30d.point_from_proportion(0.2)),
            run_time=3
        )
        self.wait(1)<ctrl63>
